raci.note: How this role participates in each process step — the RACI assignment that drives the RACI matrix. Lives in the per-process sidecar `wiki/processes/<slug>/raci.json`, keyed by role id. Each entry is `{step, level}` — the process-step id and the level (R responsible, A accountable, C consulted, I informed). A role carries an entry only for the steps it participates in. write_element.py / write_elements.py accept entries on the spec under `relations.raci` as either the structured `{step, level}` object or the legacy `<stepId>:<level>` string (the writer normalises). patch_element.py exposes the same field as `--list raci <step:level, ...>`. Across all roles, every step needs at least one R and exactly one A.

template.heading: Responsibility
template.purpose: What the role owns in the process, stated in one clear sentence.

template.heading: In this process
template.purpose: The specific steps and controls this role performs in this process.
